name: Alexandre SEPULVEDA de DIETRICH
headline: Data Scientist and Business Analytics MS candidate
targetRoleLine: Seeking Summer 2026 Data Science, AI Engineering, and ML internships in NYC
about: I build practical data and AI systems: anomaly detection for finance teams, optimization models for public-policy problems, and product-grade ML tools with modern web stacks.
heroProofBullets: Built an accounting data quality and anomaly detection pipeline at CHANEL Europe.
Shipped an end-to-end AI decision-support MVP with FastAPI, Next.js, and voice workflows.
impactHighlights: Built production-grade data pipelines and anomaly detection workflows for finance teams at CHANEL Europe
Shipped AI product MVPs and developer tools with FastAPI, Next.js, and LLM orchestration
Built optimization and simulation models for childcare access and healthcare appointment dynamics

projects.title: Tomorrow You
projects.summary: Voice-first AI decision exploration app that turns a user's dilemma into branching future personas they can interview.
projects.result: Built and deployed a working FastAPI + Next.js MVP with text and voice onboarding plus branchable future-self conversations.
highlights: Structured an end-to-end product flow from onboarding interview through branching conversations.
Connected LLM-driven profile extraction and future generation with a production-style API surface.
Added a deployed frontend experience to make the concept reviewable outside the repo.

projects.title: HelpfulLens
projects.summary: Yelp review helpfulness modeling pipeline with sentiment-enriched features, reproducible data prep, and exploratory analysis helpers.
projects.result: Built an end-to-end Python workflow from raw Yelp dumps through feature engineering, EDA, and modeling artifacts.
highlights: Built ingest and cleaning steps for Yelp review data without heavy platform dependencies.
Added sentiment-enriched feature engineering with lightweight and transformer-based options.
Packaged the work as a reproducible applied-ML repo with reports and notebooks.

projects.title: Childcare Deserts NYC
projects.summary: Optimization project for reducing NYC childcare deserts by combining facility expansions with new-site planning under cost and spacing constraints.
projects.result: Built a seven-stage notebook pipeline with ideal and realistic planning scenarios and exported report-ready tables and figures.
highlights: Formulated the planning problem as a staged optimization workflow with shared cleaning and parameter estimation.
Compared benchmark and realistic deployment scenarios using structured notebook stages.
Produced report artifacts grounded in exported solution tables, figures, and solver logs.

projects.title: Codebase Analyzer
projects.summary: WIP developer tool that ingests a repository and produces architecture diagrams, structure reports, onboarding explanations, and starter tasks.
projects.result: Current MVP spans a Fastify API and lightweight web UI for local or GitHub repo inputs.
highlights: Targets a real onboarding problem: helping engineers understand unfamiliar codebases quickly.
Combines repo crawling, artifact generation, and a lightweight review UI in one monorepo.
Strong fit for the portfolio as an active AI tooling build, but it still needs polish to move into the featured set.

projects.title: Appointment Scheduling Dynamics
projects.summary: Healthcare operations research sandbox for simulating appointment backlogs, delay-sensitive no-shows, and policy tradeoffs.
projects.result: Includes reusable simulation modules, policy comparisons, and synthesized notes from queueing literature.
highlights: Wraps queueing-model ideas from the literature in reusable Python modules and tests.
Useful portfolio evidence for simulation, policy comparison, and research synthesis work.
Needs a proper README before it should move higher in the portfolio.

projects.title: LinkedIn Note Copilot
projects.summary: Chrome extension plus FastAPI backend for generating personalized LinkedIn connection notes from the current profile page.
projects.result: Built a working browser-extension workflow with configurable profile inputs and OpenAI-backed note generation.
highlights: Combines browser automation ergonomics with a clean backend generation service.
Portfolio-relevant because it shows practical shipping instincts around workflow automation.
Should stay secondary until the parent repo is split or renamed to match the subproject.

projects.title: DNA Plasmid Closure with Genetic Algorithms
projects.summary: Evolutionary computation project that learns dinucleotide rotation parameters so 3D DNA trajectories close into plasmid loops.
projects.result: Implemented the genetic algorithm, 3D trajectory model, visualization tooling, and packaged docs/tests.
highlights: Strong evidence of mathematical modeling and scientific computing depth.
Useful portfolio support for optimization and experimental workflow design.
Best presented as coursework or research engineering rather than a product build.

caseStudies.title: Chanel Europe Analytics Pipeline
caseStudies.subtitle: Data Scientist Intern, Advanced Analytics and Data Science · CHANEL Europe
caseStudies.summary: Designed a mixed-type outlier detection approach for accounting transactions with hierarchical business categories in an SAP context.
context: Built during an internship in CHANEL Europe's Advanced Analytics and Data Science team.
Project served finance stakeholders who needed stronger trust in SAP-driven accounting data.
Because the underlying production data and workflows are confidential, shared artifacts focus on the method.
problem: Global thresholds were not sufficient because many anomalies were only abnormal within a specific categorical branch.
Some risky transactions had normal-looking amounts but suspicious combinations of categorical attributes.
constraints: Data mixed numeric amounts with high-cardinality hierarchical categorical fields from business systems.
Outputs had to remain interpretable and easy to triage in finance review workflows.
approach: Built two complementary detectors: one for amount anomalies conditioned on transaction context, and one for anomalous categorical combinations.
Used hierarchy-aware categorical representations so parent-child category structure remained informative.
Generated ranked anomaly candidates with clear reason signals to support analyst investigation.
results: Delivered a reusable algorithmic workflow for mixed-type outlier detection in hierarchical accounting data.
Published a technical note summarizing the method: Combination Outlier Detection.

media.alt: Placeholder visual for CHANEL Europe analytics pipeline case study.
media.caption: Supporting method details are available in the Combination Outlier Detection note.

nextImprovements: Add formal model and data drift monitoring dashboards.
Package reusable quality checks for faster rollout across adjacent finance datasets.

caseStudies.title: Forvia Multi-Sensor Localization Research
caseStudies.subtitle: Research Project · CentraleSupélec L2S Lab and Forvia
caseStudies.summary: Research project on multi-sensor localization using camera-derived priors and array measurements with an optimal-transport-based fusion approach.
context: Built in a collaboration between CentraleSupélec L2S Lab and Forvia.
Project explored whether lower-cost sensing could support robust localization in controlled conditions.
problem: Single-sensor signals were not sufficiently robust for stable localization on their own.
The team needed a fusion prototype that could combine a camera prior with array-based measurements.
constraints: Sensor alignment and synchronization had to remain stable across test conditions.
Camera priors and array likelihoods had different noise and resolution characteristics.
Prototype scope focused on controlled test-area validation rather than full production deployment.
approach: Built a camera-derived spatial prior from image-processing outputs.
Generated spatial likelihood maps from array measurements and fused them with an optimal-transport-based objective.
Validated localization behavior through controlled experiments and shared findings with partners.
results: Produced a working multi-sensor localization pipeline for controlled experiments.
Authored a research report documenting the setup, method, and findings.

media.alt: Placeholder visual for Forvia multi-sensor localization case study.
media.caption: Research report is available in project artifacts.

nextImprovements: Evaluate robustness under additional lighting and motion conditions.
Add confidence scoring and failure-case analysis for each tracking sequence.
